title: ProjectMind: An Evidence-Frontier and Authority-Governed Project Continuity Architecture for AI Coding Agents
description:
Problem. AI coding agents retrieve and rewrite project knowledge while operating on stale, weakly supported, or unauthorized context. Graphs, RAG memory, and tool hosts can look current while the underlying frontier has drifted past the last human-authorized commit boundary.

Gap. Existing code graphs, MCP hosts, IDE memory features, and test-impact analysis rarely bind a commit-scoped Evidence Frontier to an authority ledger and Continuity Receipt that fail closed on contract mismatch, unauthorized actors, staleness, incompleteness, or unpredicted observed deltas.

Proposed solution. ProjectMind computes a canonical Evidence Frontier per commit, maintains multi-dimension claims, compiles Change Consequence Contracts, reconciles observed deltas against predicted consequences, and emits Continuity Receipts with fail-closed verdicts (CONTINUITY_OK, CONTRACT_MISMATCH, AUTHORITY_VIOLATION, and related states).

Key evidence (v5.0.0). Importable library from projectmind import ProjectMindEngine; Reality Gate PASS 8/8; benchmarks; mutation testing ≥90%; alternative-implementation replication; numeric real-world billing-module mismatch scenario; stress tests to 2000 frontier nodes. Companion v4.0 blueprint content is preserved (NO SHRINK).

Honest limits. Not production agent infrastructure, not peer reviewed, not independently third-party replicated, not a Freedom-to-Operate opinion, and not a substitute for tests or human review. Concept DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21364197. Released for evaluation, citation, and attempted falsification. PDF regeneration may still be required.
notes: Independent Research Publication No. 4. v5.0.0. Concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.21364197. PDF REGENERATION NEEDED. Not peer reviewed. Not production. Generative AI tools assisted preparation.
